config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap jadi batas utama; kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu ditempatkan di atasnya, mudah sekali disalahgunakan oleh negara atau mayoritas untuk menekan orang yang lemah. Dalam konteks Indonesia, saya paham pentingnya gotong royong dan kepentingan bersama, tetapi itu tidak boleh mengorbankan kebebasan, martabat, dan perlindungan hukum setiap orang. Kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai tanpa meniadakan hak dasar individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support putting society’s interests above individual rights as a general rule. From an American perspective, that kind of claim makes me worry about governments or majorities using “the common good” as a blank check to trample speech, privacy, due process, and minority rights. Society absolutely has legitimate needs, but in a free country those needs should be balanced through law, not used to override basic individual protections whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Dari pengalaman Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal justru kelompok kecil atau orang biasa yang paling mudah dirugikan. Gotong royong dan rasa kebersamaan memang nilai kuat, tetapi kalau hak dasar seperti kebebasan, privasi, dan perlindungan hukum dikorbankan, itu bukan masyarakat yang sehat melainkan mayoritas yang terlalu dominan. Yang benar menurut saya adalah menyeimbangkan keduanya, dengan hak individu sebagai pagar agar kepentingan bersama tidak berubah jadi alat penindasan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general principle. From an American point of view, that wording is dangerous because it invites governments and majorities to justify almost anything as being for the common good, even when it violates speech, privacy, due process, or minority protections. I do think society has real legitimate needs, but those needs should be pursued within strong legal limits, not by treating individual rights as disposable. If the rights of the person are not the baseline, then "society" can become just a polite word for power.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, dan yang paling sering kena justru orang biasa atau kelompok kecil yang lemah. Gotong royong itu penting, tetapi kalau kebebasan, privasi, dan perlindungan hukum dikorbankan, hasilnya bukan masyarakat yang sehat melainkan kekuasaan mayoritas yang terlalu dominan. Menurut saya, kepentingan bersama harus dicapai dengan membatasi kekuasaan, bukan dengan menjadikan hak individu sebagai sesuatu yang bisa dikesampingkan kapan saja.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should have blanket priority over individual rights. From an American perspective, that idea makes me wary because history shows how easily “the public good” becomes a slogan for censorship, surveillance, and trampling minority rights, especially when the people affected have little power. I do think there are moments where society can impose narrow limits, like public health rules or laws against harm, but those limits have to be tightly justified and legally constrained. If individual rights are treated as optional, then society stops being a community of free people and starts becoming a system where the powerful decide whose rights matter.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
